results.rendered_html:
prati-√dhā P. Ā. -dadhāti, -dhatte (Ved. inf. prati-dhātave), to put on or in or near or back, return, restore (loc. or dat.),  RV. ;  AV. ;  Br. ;
to adjust (an arrow), aim,  ib. ;
to put to the lips (for drinking),  RV. iv, 27, 5 ;
to put down (the feet), step out,  Br. ;
to offer, present,  AitBr. ;
to use, employ,  Śatr. ;
to restrain,  BhP. ;
(Ā.) to commence, begin, approach,  RV. ;  AV.

results.rendered_html:
pratidhā 3 U.
1 To place or lay on.
2 To return, restore.
3 To fix (as an arrow).
4 To offer, present.
5 To use, employ.
6 To begin, commence.
7 To draw near, approach (as the night); (mostly Vedic in these senses).
